TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kavalan, story: KavNr-pear_banday

S.FORM: yau taytaanku a a siasin tangi nani.
zaqis tu paRin ta muluan qimulu.
S.TRANSL: (He) climbed up the tree to pluck oranges.

S.FORM: yau usiq a tazungan siqaytun nani simqaRiway.
S.TRANSL: 有個女孩騎車，然後(跟他)相撞。
There was a girl riding a bicycle and then collided with (the child).

S.FORM: sukaw lazan msuRaw ti a sunis ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 路不好，(所以)小孩跌倒了。
The road was uneven, (so) the child fell.

S.FORM: tu tmibuq ti a mulu ‘nay tamelanayan.
S.TRANSL: The oranges fell onto the ground.

S.FORM: tawian tangayauanna nani yau a m kinspat a sunis a mawRat.
S.TRANSL: 前面那邊有四個小孩在玩。
In the front there, there were four children playing around.

S.FORM: taytaanna tu msuRaw qaqaytun ay sunis tu.
S.TRANSL: 他們看到那個騎腳踏車的小孩跌倒。
(They) saw that the child who rode a bike fell.

S.FORM: ngid tungaw tu mulu talepawanna nani.
S.TRANSL: (The child) wanted to send those oranges to his home.

S.FORM: pangmuanna pizi taqaytunan ya mulu ‘nay nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們幫忙把橘子放回車上。
They helped put those oranges onto the car.
他們再扶那個小孩上車。
They lifted the child onto the bike again.

S.FORM: patuRuzanna sunis ma sunisna mawRat ay tu halo zinna qmeRas nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們背著那個玩耍的小孩,大聲地說哈囉。
They carried the child who was playing and said ‘hello’ loudly.
He went to those children again.

S.FORM: nani ‘nay sayi nani nanzuzusaanna ti qman tu mulu ‘nay.
S.TRANSL: 他們可以分半吃那些橘子。
They could share the oranges with one another.
